reviews.review_description:
The Climb Adventure is an ambitious climbing game that sits nicely between pure simulation and arcade gameplay.

This indie title confidently steps into a space dominated by AAA experiences like The Climb and The Climb 2. It offers many varied levels with different atmospheres, keeping each climb fresh and engaging.

The execution is great, and the sensation of climbing and pushing toward the summit feels very natural. A nice touch is how some routes lead to dead ends, forcing you to backtrack — just like real-life climbing.

It’s quick to jump into, fun, and highly immersive from the start.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this thoughtful review.
It means a lot to us that you fully understood the core of The Climb Adventure — the balance between simulation and arcade, the natural climbing feel, and the intentional route design.

reviews.review_title: Climbing Fun with a Few Frustrations.
reviews.review_description:
It is a really enjoyable  experience that captures the thrill of climbing surprisingly well. The environments have varied routes that keep things interesting, and there’s a real sense of height and risk that adds to the excitement. With the crumbling rocks and wind noises adding to the immersion

The controls are mostly intuitive, and once you get into the rhythm of climbing, it feels rewarding and engaging. However, one issue that did stand out was the jump mechanic. Using the A button to leap across ledges felt a bit janky and inconsistent at times—sometimes it worked perfectly, other times it felt hit-and-miss, which can be frustrating when you’re mid-climb and relying on precision.

Despite that, it’s still a very enjoyable game overall and well worth playing if you like VR adventure or climbing-style experiences. With a bit more polish on the jumping, this could easily be a 5-star title.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed review! 😊
We’re really happy you enjoyed the climbing, environments, and immersion. The feedback about the jump using the A button is very valuable for us — we’re already looking into improvements to make it more consistent and precise during climbs.

reviews.review_title: A climbing game to rival the best
reviews.review_description:
We all know about The Climb and climb 2, made on a much smaller budget but just as much fun, the climbing is accurate and work really well, I do prefer smooth turning so this would be a great addition, the various climbs are pretty cool an imaginative, from ice to volcano, lots of areas to chose from, this does need more visibility as this is better than some AAA games on bigger budgets.
Well done I now need to climb the leaderboards.

developer_response.body:
We’re really happy to see our game being compared with major references in the genre — it means a lot to our team. We’ve put a lot of dedication into making the climbing feel accurate and fun.

We also appreciate your suggestion about smooth turning and your feedback on the variety of environments. Comments like yours help the project improve and gain more visibility.

Thank you for supporting the game, and good luck climbing the leaderboards! 🧗‍♂️🎮
